mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or the signal fails to predict direction with statistical significance in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand expansion, which drives corporate earnings expectations and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 60-day returns in > 60% of occurrences, or the interaction term loses predictive power when controlling for monetary policy shifts.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which translates to higher corporate profit margins and positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days during post-recession recovery phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 20-day returns during recovery periods, or the mechanism reverses direction when inflation exceeds 3% annually.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls indicate robust economic activity and rising household income, which supports equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero or negative correlation with 20-day forward returns across multiple rolling 2-year windows, or Rank IC remains below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly inflation rate exceeding 0.4% triggers expectations of tighter monetary policy and margin compression, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 5-day returns in > 65% of high-inflation trigger months, or the inflation threshold fails to separate winning and losing periods with p < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly growth in real GDP and monthly retail sales confirms synchronized demand expansion across production and consumption sectors, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined GDP and retail sales signal yields negative 20-day returns in > 50% of cases, or the interaction effect vanishes when FEDFUNDS changes by more than 0.25%.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a late-cycle expansion signals capacity constraints and impending economic slowdown, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during late-cycle phases fail to predict negative 60-day returns, or the signal produces positive returns in > 55% of triggered instances.
